para_trans: Grievance Redressal Committee to take a final decision on the eligibility of encroachers for removal and rehabilitation of encroachments on forest land of Sanjay Gandhi National Park after determining their eligibility / ineligibility.
regarding the establishment.
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department,
Government Decision No: WAP 04.26/प R. No.932 / F-1
Mantralaya, Mumbai 400032.
Date: April 24, 2026.
Reference: 1. The High Court, Bombay in Writ Petition No. In 305/95. The date of the court order 07.05.1997.
Revenue and Forest Department, Government Decision No. 08.13/प R.No. 301 / F-1, dated 08.01.2014.
3. Ma. Public Interest Litigation No. Contempt Petition No. 48/2023 and Writ Petition No. 305/95. In the joint hearing of 9237/20223, Md. The court has given
Order date 14.01.2025.

4. Department of Revenue & Forest, Government Decision No. WLP 04.2025/प R.No.102 / F-1, D. 09.04.2025 5. Revenue and Forest Department, Government Decision No. WLP 04.2025/प R.No. 102 / F-1, dated 10.04.2026

Preamble: Repeal of encroachment on forest land of Sanjay Gandhi National Park B. Eviction of ineligible encroachers Reference No. 1 for the area of Sanjay Gandhi National Park in Mumbai Suburban District and Sanjay Gandhi National Park in Thane District for rehabilitation. Two committees have been constituted under the Government Decision No. 5.

Reference No. In pursuance of the decision of the Committees constituted under Government Resolution No. 5, the Government was contemplating setting up of Grievance Redressal Committee to redress the grievances / grievances received from individuals / parties and take a final decision on the eligibility of the encroacher concerned.

Government Decision: Complaints / applications received from individuals / parties after determination of eligibility / ineligibility of encroachers for eviction and rehabilitation of encroachments on forest land of Sanjay Gandhi National Park.
Grievance Redressal Committee is being constituted as follows to take a final decision on their eligibility by making redressal within prescribed limits.

2. Also Reference No. If the encroachers concerned are not satisfied after the qualification has been determined by the committee constituted under section 5, they may lodge their formal complaint against the decision of the said committee with the said Grievance Redressal Committee within a period of 30 days. No complaint / application will be entertained outside the mentioned period.

Necessary action in accordance with the application / complaint received by the said Grievance Redressal Committee shall be taken by the Committee concerned within the next 90 days from the date of filing of the complaint.
The decision taken by the Grievance Redressal Committee in relation to the determination of eligibility shall be final.

4. Assistant Conservator of Forests (Technical), Director of Forest Conservancy B, Sanjay Gandhi National Park, Borivali is being appointed as the coordinator for the functioning of the committee constituted for the said purpose.

5. Members of the Grievance Redressal Committee being constituted to determine eligibility under the rehabilitation of encroachers in Sanjay Gandhi National Park will be given a monthly stipend of Rs. A stipend of ₹50,000 / - is being fixed, which should be paid by the Sanjay Gandhi National Park administration.

The Government Decision is available on the Government of Maharashtra website www.-maharashtra.gov.in with the computer number 202604241257344819. The order is being certified with a digital signature
By order and in the name of the Governor of Maharashtra,
